search_text: Applications of physics informed neural operators 物理知识神经算子的应用 We present an end-to-end framework to learn partial differential equations that brings together initial data production, selection of boundary conditions, and the use of physics-informed neural operators to solve partial differential equations that are ubiquitous in the study and modeling of physics phenomena. We first demonstrate that our methods reproduce the accuracy and performance of other neural operators published elsewhere in the literature to learn the 1D wave equation and the 1D Burgers equation. Thereafter, we apply our physics-informed neural operators to learn new types of equations, including the 2D Burgers equation in the scalar, inviscid and vector types. Finally, we show that our approach is also applicable to learn the physics of the 2D linear and nonlinear shallow water equations, which involve three coupled partial differential equations. We release our artificial intelligence surrogates and scientific software to produce initial data and boundary conditions to study a broad range of physically motivated scenarios. We provide the source code, an interactive website to visualize the predictions of our physics informed neural operators, and a tutorial for their use at the Data and Learning Hub for Science. 我们提出了一个学习偏微分方程的端到端框架，该框架汇集了初始数据生成、边界条件的选择以及使用物理信息神经算子来求解在物理现象的研究和建模中普遍存在的偏微分方程。我们首先证明我们的方法重现了文献中其他地方发布的其他神经算子的准确性和性能，以学习一维波动方程和一维伯格斯方程。此后，我们应用基于物理的神经算子来学习新类型的方程，包括标量、无粘性和矢量类型的 2D Burgers 方程。最后，我们表明我们的方法也适用于学习二维线性和非线性浅水方程的物理学，其中涉及三个耦合偏微分方程。我们发布人工智能替代品和科学软件来生成初始数据和边界条件，以研究各种物理动机场景。我们提供源代码、一个交互式网站，用于可视化基于物理的神经算子的预测，以及在科学数据和学习中心使用它们的教程。 本文提出一种端到端框架，结合物理信息神经算子学习偏微分方程，应用于多种物理场景。 Shawn G. Rosofsky,Hani Al Majed,E. A. Huerta physics.comp-ph,astro-ph.IM,cs.AI,cs.LG,gr-qc

authors_text: Alp Yurtsever,Tolga Birdal,Vladislav Golyanik
title: Q-FW: A Hybrid Classical-Quantum Frank-Wolfe for Quadratic Binary Optimization
title_zh: Q-FW：用于二次二元优化的混合经典量子 Frank-Wolfe
abstract: We present a hybrid classical-quantum framework based on the Frank-Wolfe algorithm, Q-FW, for solving quadratic, linearly-constrained, binary optimization problems on quantum annealers (QA). The computational premise of quantum computers has cultivated the re-design of various existing vision problems into quantum-friendly forms. Experimental QA realizations can solve a particular non-convex problem known as the quadratic unconstrained binary optimization (QUBO). Yet a naive-QUBO cannot take into account the restrictions on the parameters. To introduce additional structure in the parameter space, researchers have crafted ad-hoc solutions incorporating (linear) constraints in the form of regularizers. However, this comes at the expense of a hyper-parameter, balancing the impact of regularization. To date, a true constrained solver of quadratic binary optimization (QBO) problems has lacked. Q-FW first reformulates constrained-QBO as a copositive program (CP), then employs Frank-Wolfe iterations to solve CP while satisfying linear (in)equality constraints. This procedure unrolls the original constrained-QBO into a set of unconstrained QUBOs all of which are solved, in a sequel, on a QA. We use D-Wave Advantage QA to conduct synthetic and real experiments on two important computer vision problems, graph matching and permutation synchronization, which demonstrate that our approach is effective in alleviating the need for an explicit regularization coefficient.

The computational premise of quantum computers has cultivated the re-design of various existing vision problems into quantum-friendly forms. To introduce additional structure in the parameter space, researchers have crafted ad-hoc solutions incorporating (linear) constraints in the form of regularizers. However, this comes at the expense of a hyper-parameter, balancing the impact of regularization. To date, a true constrained solver of quadratic binary optimization (QBO) problems has lacked. Q-FW first reformulates constrained-QBO as a copositive program (CP), then employs Frank-Wolfe iterations to solve CP while satisfying linear (in)equality constraints. This procedure unrolls the original constrained-QBO into a set of unconstrained QUBOs all of which are solved, in a sequel, on a QA. We use D-Wave Advantage QA to conduct synthetic and real experiments on two important computer vision problems, graph matching and permutation synchronization, which demonstrate that our approach is effective in alleviating the need for an explicit regularization coefficient.
abstract_zh: We present a hybrid classical-quantum framework based on the Frank-Wolfe algorithm, Q-FW, for solving quadratic, linearly-constrained, binary optimization problems on quantum annealers (QA).量子计算机的计算前提促使人们将各种现有的视觉问题重新设计成量子友好的形式。实验 QA 实现可以解决称为二次无约束二元优化 (QUBO) 的特定非凸问题。然而，naive-QUBO 无法考虑参数的限制。 To introduce additional structure in the parameter space, researchers have crafted ad-hoc solutions incorporating (linear) constraints in the form of regularizers.然而，这是以超参数为代价的，平衡正则化的影响。迄今为止，还缺乏二次二元优化 (QBO) 问题的真正约束求解器。 Q-FW first reformulates constrained-QBO as a copositive program (CP), then employs Frank-Wolfe iterations to solve CP while satisfying linear (in)equality constraints.此过程将原始受约束 QBO 展开为一组无约束 QUBO，所有这些都在后续的 QA 中得到解决。 We use D-Wave Advantage QA to conduct synthetic and real experiments on two important computer vision problems, graph matching and permutation synchronization, which demonstrate that our approach is effective in alleviating the need for an explicit regularization coefficient.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合经典与量子计算的Frank-Wolfe算法框架Q-FW，用于在量子退火器上求解带线性约束的二次二进制优化问题。
AI.motivation: 现有量子二进制优化方法（如QUBO）难以有效处理约束条件，依赖正则化超参数导致性能不稳定，缺乏真正的约束求解器。
AI.method: 将约束二次二进制优化（QBO）问题转化为共正定规划（CP），通过Frank-Wolfe迭代求解并满足线性约束，最终将问题分解为一系列无约束QUBO在量子退火器上求解。
AI.result: 在D-Wave Advantage量子退火器上针对图匹配和排列同步问题的实验表明，该方法有效降低了对显式正则化系数的依赖性。
AI.conclusion: Q-FW为量子退火器处理带约束的二次优化问题提供了新框架，推动了量子计算在计算机视觉等领域的应用潜力。

authors_text: Youngmin Kim,Richard Allmendinger,Manuel López-Ibáñez
title: Are Evolutionary Algorithms Safe Optimizers?
title_zh: 进化算法是安全的优化器吗？
comment: To be published in proceedings of Genetic and Evolutionary Computation Conference (GECCO 22), July 9-13, 2022, Boston, MA, USA. ACM, New York, NY, USA, 9 pages. https://doi.org/10.1145/3512290.3528818
comments: To be published in proceedings of Genetic and Evolutionary Computation Conference (GECCO 22), July 9-13, 2022, Boston, MA, USA. ACM, New York, NY, USA, 9 pages. https://doi.org/10.1145/3512290.3528818
abstract: We consider a type of constrained optimization problem, where the violation of a constraint leads to an irrevocable loss, such as breakage of a valuable experimental resource/platform or loss of human life. Such problems are referred to as safe optimization problems (SafeOPs). While SafeOPs have received attention in the machine learning community in recent years, there was little interest in the evolutionary computation (EC) community despite some early attempts between 2009 and 2011. Moreover, there is a lack of acceptable guidelines on how to benchmark different algorithms for SafeOPs, an area where the EC community has significant experience in. Driven by the need for more efficient algorithms and benchmark guidelines for SafeOPs, the objective of this paper is to reignite the interest of this problem class in the EC community. To achieve this we (i) provide a formal definition of SafeOPs and contrast it to other types of optimization problems that the EC community is familiar with, (ii) investigate the impact of key SafeOP parameters on the performance of selected safe optimization algorithms, (iii) benchmark EC against state-of-the-art safe optimization algorithms from the machine learning community, and (iv) provide an open-source Python framework to replicate and extend our work.
summary: We consider a type of constrained optimization problem, where the violation of a constraint leads to an irrevocable loss, such as breakage of a valuable experimental resource/platform or loss of human life. Such problems are referred to as safe optimization problems (SafeOPs). While SafeOPs have received attention in the machine learning community in recent years, there was little interest in the evolutionary computation (EC) community despite some early attempts between 2009 and 2011. Moreover, there is a lack of acceptable guidelines on how to benchmark different algorithms for SafeOPs, an area where the EC community has significant experience in. Driven by the need for more efficient algorithms and benchmark guidelines for SafeOPs, the objective of this paper is to reignite the interest of this problem class in the EC community. To achieve this we (i) provide a formal definition of SafeOPs and contrast it to other types of optimization problems that the EC community is familiar with, (ii) investigate the impact of key SafeOP parameters on the performance of selected safe optimization algorithms, (iii) benchmark EC against state-of-the-art safe optimization algorithms from the machine learning community, and (iv) provide an open-source Python framework to replicate and extend our work.
abstract_zh: 我们考虑一种约束优化问题，其中违反约束会导致不可挽回的损失，例如有价值的实验资源/平台的破坏或人员生命的损失。此类问题称为安全优化问题（SafeOP）。尽管 SafeOPs 近年来在机器学习社区中受到关注，但进化计算 (EC) 社区尽管在 2009 年至 2011 年间进行了一些早期尝试，但对如何对不同算法进行基准测试缺乏可接受的指南，而 EC 社区在这一领域拥有丰富的经验。由于需要更高效的算法和 SafeOPs 基准指南，本文的目的是重新激发 EC 社区对此类问题的兴趣。为了实现这一目标，我们 (i) 提供 SafeOP 的正式定义，并将其与 EC 社区熟悉的其他类型的优化问题进行对比，(ii) 研究关键 SafeOP 参数对所选安全优化算法性能的影响，(iii) 针对机器学习社区最先进的安全优化算法对 EC 进行基准测试，以及 (iv) 提供开源 Python 框架来复制和扩展我们的工作。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在重新激发进化计算（EC）社区对安全优化问题（SafeOPs）的关注，并提供基准测试指南和开源框架。
AI.motivation: 由于安全优化问题（SafeOPs）在机器学习领域受到关注，但进化计算（EC）社区缺乏相关基准测试指南，亟需更高效的算法和评估标准。
AI.method: 通过形式化定义SafeOPs，分析关键参数影响，对比EC与机器学习算法，并开发开源Python框架。
AI.result: 实验结果表明EC方法在SafeOPs中的有效性，并提供了可复现的开源工具。
AI.conclusion: 本文呼吁进化计算社区重视安全优化问题，并通过基准测试和工具推动相关研究进展。

search_text: Are Evolutionary Algorithms Safe Optimizers? 进化算法是安全的优化器吗？ We consider a type of constrained optimization problem, where the violation of a constraint leads to an irrevocable loss, such as breakage of a valuable experimental resource/platform or loss of human life. Such problems are referred to as safe optimization problems (SafeOPs). While SafeOPs have received attention in the machine learning community in recent years, there was little interest in the evolutionary computation (EC) community despite some early attempts between 2009 and 2011. Moreover, there is a lack of acceptable guidelines on how to benchmark different algorithms for SafeOPs, an area where the EC community has significant experience in. Driven by the need for more efficient algorithms and benchmark guidelines for SafeOPs, the objective of this paper is to reignite the interest of this problem class in the EC community. To achieve this we (i) provide a formal definition of SafeOPs and contrast it to other types of optimization problems that the EC community is familiar with, (ii) investigate the impact of key SafeOP parameters on the performance of selected safe optimization algorithms, (iii) benchmark EC against state-of-the-art safe optimization algorithms from the machine learning community, and (iv) provide an open-source Python framework to replicate and extend our work. 我们考虑一种约束优化问题，其中违反约束会导致不可挽回的损失，例如有价值的实验资源/平台的破坏或人员生命的损失。此类问题称为安全优化问题（SafeOP）。尽管 SafeOPs 近年来在机器学习社区中受到关注，但进化计算 (EC) 社区尽管在 2009 年至 2011 年间进行了一些早期尝试，但对如何对不同算法进行基准测试缺乏可接受的指南，而 EC 社区在这一领域拥有丰富的经验。由于需要更高效的算法和 SafeOPs 基准指南，本文的目的是重新激发 EC 社区对此类问题的兴趣。为了实现这一目标，我们 (i) 提供 SafeOP 的正式定义，并将其与 EC 社区熟悉的其他类型的优化问题进行对比，(ii) 研究关键 SafeOP 参数对所选安全优化算法性能的影响，(iii) 针对机器学习社区最先进的安全优化算法对 EC 进行基准测试，以及 (iv) 提供开源 Python 框架来复制和扩展我们的工作。 本文旨在重新激发进化计算（EC）社区对安全优化问题（SafeOPs）的关注，并提供基准测试指南和开源框架。 Youngmin Kim,Richard Allmendinger,Manuel López-Ibáñez cs.NE,cs.LG,eess.SY
